matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation. Variant prediction rules receive the decoded sample GT, zygosity, ALT dosage, and site REF -> ALT definition; curated allele rules take precedence only when the sample genotype carries the relevant allele dosage.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.
seeded_markers: MS4A2 immune-response variation model (MS4A2 allergy and inflammation pathway model)
MS4A2 regulatory expression context model (MS4A2 expression and methylation-context model)
concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a MS4A2 allergy and inflammation thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to IgE receptor biology, mast-cell and eosinophil activation, cytokine and chemokine signaling, NF-kappaB control, and JAK-STAT inflammatory regulation. Treat the result as candidate pathway evidence until exact allele, zygosity, phenotype, and external classification are reviewed.

variant_prediction_rules.common_name: MS4A2 allergy and inflammation pathway model
variant_prediction_rules.prediction: GT-confirmed MS4A2 immune-response variation model dosage suggests this sample carries the MS4A2 prediction context described by the gene-level thesis. The variant observed in this sample suggests a MS4A2 allergy and inflammation thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to IgE receptor biology, mast-cell and eosinophil activation, cytokine and chemokine signaling, NF-kappaB control, and JAK-STAT inflammatory regulation. Treat the result as candidate pathway evidence until exact allele, zygosity, phenotype, and external classification are reviewed. This specific marker is curated as MS4A2 allergy and inflammation pathway model. MS4A2 immune-response variation model is curated as a MS4A2 marker for Allergy, asthma, atopy, and inflammatory disease context, Mast-cell, eosinophil, cytokine, and chemokine pathway biology. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the IgE receptor biology, mast-cell and eosinophil activation, cytokine and chemokine signaling, NF-kappaB control, and JAK-STAT inflammatory regulation pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_prediction_rules.sample_change_template: This sample matched MS4A2 immune-response variation model at site definition {change} with GT {gt_raw} decoded as {genotype} ({zygosity}). Anchor the MS4A2 prediction to genotype dosage {allele_dosage} rather than REF -> ALT presence alone. Dosage-specific conclusions should be softened when GT, depth, balance, or genotype quality are uncertain.
variant_prediction_rules.basis: Can modify or tag IgE receptor biology, mast-cell and eosinophil activation, cytokine and chemokine signaling, NF-kappaB control, and JAK-STAT inflammatory regulation.

cases.label: Gene variant found
cases.prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a MS4A2 allergy and inflammation thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to IgE receptor biology, mast-cell and eosinophil activation, cytokine and chemokine signaling, NF-kappaB control, and JAK-STAT inflammatory regulation. Treat the result as candidate pathway evidence until exact allele, zygosity, phenotype, and external classification are reviewed. An observed MS4A2 variant places this sample in the curated MS4A2 research context. The local MS4A2 bundle is oriented toward allergy and inflammation. Use it for pathway triage, phenotype review, and candidate-gene context; external variant classification is required before clinical interpretation. MS4A2 variants can alter, tag, or contextualize IgE receptor biology, mast-cell and eosinophil activation, cytokine and chemokine signaling, NF-kappaB control, and JAK-STAT inflammatory regulation.

cases.label: Gene variant found + high whitelist mean beta
cases.prediction: When a MS4A2 variant is paired with high methylation in the whitelist mean beta, the sample best fits a combined regulatory-context thesis. This pattern leans toward stronger regulatory restraint or reduced local accessibility around the locus.
